mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP growth was 2.5% in Q4 2018 annualized
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 YoY change against 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or correlation is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, monetary tightening reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate was 2.27 at latest observation, approaching threshold
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in at least 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5, or Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level against weekly returns is -0.02 or higher over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a non-accommodative federal funds rate (policy inertia) squeezes real earnings growth and raises equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.0 and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with positive real interest rates
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in over 60% of activation events, or the interaction term in a linear regression of returns on CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS has a t-stat less than 2 in magnitude over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, rising industrial production signals demand overheating and subsequent margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production was 109.4 in November 2018, consistent with expansion
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of INDPRO growth on 60-day forward returns is greater than -0.03 or if the negative directional hit rate is below 55% in tightening regime windows over any 3-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer spending momentum, which immediately boosts earnings expectations and supports short-term positive S&P 500 returns within a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales rose 0.5% month-over-month in November 2018
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or the average return conditioned on positive retail sales surprises is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 150,000 monthly additions, it signals weakening labor demand and impending economic slowdown, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls were 150,263 in December 2018, barely above threshold
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in at least half of the months when PAYEMS < 150,000, or if the average return difference between above- and below-threshold periods is not statistically negative at the 10% level over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with rising federal funds rate jointly signal a downturn in interest-rate-sensitive sectors, increasing recession risk and depressing equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts were 1256 in November 2018, near weak levels; Fed was hiking
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing weakness
failure_condition: If the joint indicator does not produce negative 60-day returns in more than two-thirds of activation periods, or if the interaction dummy in a regression has a coefficient with p > 0.10 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with a tight labor market (UNRATE below natural rate estimate of 4.5%), further declines in unemployment foreshadow wage inflation and Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.9 in December 2018, indicating very tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 4.0, or the Rank IC of UNRATE level is not negative at -0.05 or stronger over any 3-year rolling window.
